rationale: Audience overlap is strong — casual women 25-55 are the core demographic for both word puzzles and sorting games, and the "satisfaction" emotional pitch is on-brand for Marble Sort's calm/satisfying UI mood. However, the mechanic match is poor: a crossword/letter-tray hook misrepresents the physics-drop sorting loop and risks heavy install-to-IAP fall-off plus store policy flags for misleading creative. Visually the warm brown/green/yellow food palette also clashes with the lavender pastel DNA — if adapted, swap the crossword for an analogous "tap-to-place colored marbles into matching slots" satisfaction hook keeping the rapid-tap cadence but using the actual core loop.
